answers.where: Yellow Backrooms corridor facing a non-Euclidean room aperture with native proxy geometry for jambs, threshold, fixture, conduit, and landmark column
answers.action: Use the copied mark as evidence, find the missing handle, and test the locked door
answers.response: The mark appears inside the impossible room, and the runtime now draws 66 native nt_gfx overlay vertices on top of the data-driven portal-room shader
answers.reward: The player learns the space can be tested and later stabilized by fitting the found handle
answers.game_look: Native 3D liminal horror view with deeper portal room, shadowed aperture, stained wallpaper, carpet seams, minimal journal UI, and a separate geometry overlay pass

issues.text: Native proxy geometry proves a real render layer, but the room still reads as a staged shader/proxy composition rather than a realistic constructed Backrooms space
The impossible-space hook is clearer, but the screenshot still falls short of a distinctive high-quality horror game among many Backrooms games

problem: The portal proof now has real native proxy quads, but the image still lacks production-quality realistic room construction and lighting/material richness
next: Replace proxy quads and shader-authored room surfaces with richer authored mesh/material layers, or use the T0011 render-target path once the engine exports it
